Our guide highlights the top double glazing companies in the UK, offering insights into costs, benefits, and everything you need to know about installing double glazing in your home.

As the weather is getting colder in the UK, you may be one of many homeowners searching for ‘double glazing window companies near me’ and being met with thousands of results. To save you spending hours weeding out the good from the bad and the good from the great, we’ve done the heavy lifting for you.

In this guide, we’ve shortlisted the best double glazing installers in the UK, comparing their reputation, [window prices](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/how-much-do-double-glazing-windows-cost/), customer service and product quality to help you keep your house warm and insulated and your energy bills low.

## How our experts choose the best double glazing installers

### Warranties

Different window companies offer varying warranty terms. The shortest warranty featured in this guide is 10 years, which is roughly half the average lifespan of uPVC double glazing.

### Price

The dimensions and frame materials you choose all influence the cost of double glazing windows. On top of that, each provider prices their products differently. It’s worth noting there is help to cover the cost of windows in the shape of [double glazing government grants](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/government-grants-for-new-and-replacement-windows/).

### Energy efficiency

As well as increasing your property’s value, double glazing can enhance the energy efficiency of your home. Replacing your windows can save you up to £235 a year on your energy bills, depending on the energy efficiency of the windows you choose.

### Security

The companies we selected install double glazed windows accredited by Secured by Design (SBD), which means they meet police-approved security standards.

## Anglian Home Improvements: Best overall double glazing windows company

![anglian windows fitted at a property](https://duf3nm0bhk54y.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/anglian.jpg.webp)Image credit: Anglian.

### Key information

- **Guarantee options:** 10-year guarantee offered on all parts and labour and 10–15-year guarantee offered on gas-filled windows
- **Price range:** from £275 to £1,000 per window
- **Energy-efficiency rating:** B, A or A++ on double and triple glazing
- **SBD certified:** Yes, for casement, tilt and turn and Elite sliding sash windows

### Company overview

Anglian’s energy-efficient windows come in a range of styles and materials, including wood and [aluminium](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/aluminium-windows/). The company also provides [uPVC windows](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/upvc-windows-guide/), which are available in several finishes. Decorative glass options and advanced safety features – such as key locking handles, multipoint locks, excluder devices and shoot bolt locks – are also available.

In the 1960s, the company was founded as Anglian Windows, and it has since become a market leader. Anglian fits more than half a million products in the UK every year, all bearing the Made in Britain trademark. Prioritising economic and environmental sustainability, the company takes part in the Recovinyl Plus PVC recycling initiative, offering you up to £1,000 off new Anglian windows and doors when you recycle your old ones.

Anglian’s fair payment options, impressive accreditations and glowing reviews earned it excellent marks for its double glazing products and services. The company provides windows in various styles, including [tilt and turn windows](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/tilt-and-turn-windows/), casement, [sash](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/sash-windows/), flush, bay and cottage.  A range of Scandinavian-inspired styles and colours is also available, including uPVC frames with wood finishes.

### **Cost**

Like many double glazing companies, Anglian doesn’t publish its prices as each installation is tailored to the customer. The company does offer a price-match guarantee on uPVC windows, providing a like-for-like quote is presented.

Per our research, the average cost of a 600mm x 900mm window from Anglian is as follows:

| Material | Cost |
| --- | --- |
| uPVC | £275 – £630 |
| Aluminium | £525 – £675 |
| Composite | £600 – £685 |
| Wood | £890 – £1,000 |

Prices correct as of April 2026.

### **Customer testimonials**

As of March 2026, Anglian’s [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/www.anglianhome.co.uk) score stands at 4.0 out of 5 stars from over 38,000 reviews. The company does have a mixed reputation, which is visible in the reviews. 60 per cent of customers awarded it 5 stars, but a significant 21 per cent of reviews are 1 or 2 stars.

While many customers praise the wide range of window styles, the quality of the products, and professionalism of installers, common complaints revolve around communication issues and pushy sales tactics. There are also reports of missed appointments and difficulty getting in touch with the company to fix issues. Despite the mixed feedback, Anglian remains a popular choice due to its strong market presence.

### Our recommendation

Anglian’s beautifully designed bespoke products come in an wide variety of styles. However, although the company provides some financing options, some of its competitors have a more favourable annual percentage rate (APR) than Anglian’s 12.9 per cent. Some brands such as Britannia also offer interest-free credit and buy now, pay later plans. As such, we’d recommend Anglian for cash buyers.

Anglian’s safety features, especially its locking systems and highly-coveted Secured By Design (SBD) accreditations, are ideal if you’re concerned about security. The company’s wood windows and doors also hold several accreditations and licences, including Forest Stewardship Council certifications. Anglian’s quality products, accreditations, market presence and solid guarantees led us to choose this installer as the overall best double glazing company.

## Britelite Home Improvements: Most cost-effective

![britelite windows fitted at a property](https://duf3nm0bhk54y.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/britelite.jpg.webp)Image credit: Britelite.

### Key information

- **Guarantee options:** 10-year transferable guarantee
- **Price range:** from £550 per window
- **Energy-efficiency rating:** A+ on all uPVC double glazing
- **SBD certified:** Yes, on some products

### Company overview

With over 50 years in the double glazing industry, Britelite Windows focuses on energy efficiency and security, as demonstrated by its proprietary 10-point lock system and the fact that some of its products are SBD accredited.

We included Britelite in our shortlist mainly thanks to its transparent price policy, as all of its quotes include VAT and fitting costs, so there are no hidden fees.

Many online reviews suggest Britelite’s prices are more affordable than those of its competitors, and customers have also reported that their installation resulted in impressive energy savings. The company holds the British Sustainability Institute Kitemark accreditation for window installation.

### **Cost**

Britelite provided us with the average prices for a standard casement window (excluding installation and labour):

| Material | **Average cost** |
| --- | --- |
| uPVC | From £550 per window |
| Aluminium | £600 – £700 per window |
| Composite | From £1,150 per window |

Prices correct as of April 2026.

### **Customer testimonials**

Britelite receives generally positive reviews on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/www.britelitewindows.co.uk), with customers commenting on its professional, polite installers, fair prices, and their overall satisfaction with the finished product. As of March 2026, its score of 4.6 starts out of 5 consists of 82 per cent of 4- or 5-star reviews. A few negative reviews report some communication issues with the Britelite sales team, but these seem anecdotal and the company replies to all of them to attempt to resolve them.

### **Our recommendation**

If you’re on a budget and want to avoid hidden fees, Britelite Windows is a good option. Your quote is VAT-inclusive, thanks to the company’s competitive, transparent pricing, so you won’t have to worry about additional costs when making your decision. Unlike some of its competitors, Britelite receives praise in online reviews for its straightforward sales tactics.

However, Britelite’s representative APR is 12.9 per cent, and the company doesn’t offer many payment options. The company also has less choice of materials and design features compared to competitors, and it only provides a standard 10-year warranty.

### Company overview

Britannia Windows is a well-known family business that was founded in 1982. Serving customers in Kent, Sussex, Essex, Surrey and London, the company uses a network of installers to ensure quick delivery. Britannia Windows gets mixed reviews online, but it responds directly to negative reviews and has a solid reputation overall. If you’re generally busy during office hours, you may find the company’s extended contact centre service hours (Monday to Friday, 8.00am to 8.00pm) quite helpful.

If you prefer using credit, Britannia has multiple finance options and extremely flexible payment terms. The company also has an extensive range of window designs, styles and colours to choose from.

Pricing for Britannia windows is not available online, but we were able to identify a price range for their products using customer reviews. Britannia’s costs are similar to those of Anglian, and most reviewers rate Britannia’s prices as “fair” or “reasonable”.

| Material | Cost (excluding installation) |
| --- | --- |
| uPVC | £300 – £600 per window |
| Aluminium | £550 – £750 per window |

### **Customer testimonials**

As of March 2026, Britannia is rated 4.1 stars out of 5 on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/britanniawindows.co.uk). Despite the high overall rating, it’s worth noting reviews are polarised, with 64 per cent of 5-star reviews and 23 per cent of 1-star reviews.

Many homeowners rely on payment plans as new windows are a significant investment. With showrooms in convenient locations around South East England, Britannia has a reputation for friendly service and efficient installation, making it a solid all-round option. Although it’s a smaller business than some of the others on our list, Britannia still boasts an impressive range of window designs and colours.

## How to find the best window company near you

There are plenty of horror stories online about ineffective fittings, noisy vents and property damage due to a careless new window installation, so it’s important to check out the quality of the service provided by any double glazing installer.

To get a good idea of the range of prices and products on offer, collect quotes from both national and local window installers. As most companies supply similar products, focus on each provider’s reputation and customer service when making your decision. There are some advantages to choosing a local double glazing installer, such as a friend or relative being able to share their experience with the company. However, we wouldn’t recommend accepting advice from strangers in local social media groups when it comes to choosing a window installer.

Below are some things we recommend thinking about while assessing double glazing window companies.

### Look for experience

Look for a well-established company that has been in business for several years – the longer, the better. This is a good indicator of expertise and reliability, which are valuable during the installation process and for ongoing support.

### Check customer feedback

Read all the testimonials and customer reviews you can find, and check out third-party websites. While every installer will have some negative reviews, the company’s response to such criticism can help you gauge its level of professionalism. Good aftercare service is more likely with companies that are willing to correct any issues. An argumentative or defensive response, on the other hand, may be a warning sign.

### Ask to see a portfolio of work

Check if local installers have photos of their completed projects available for prospective customers to look at. Local companies may even be able to tell you which houses they’ve worked on in your neighbourhood, allowing you to see the quality of the windows and installation for yourself. Visit the company’s website and look for a gallery or portfolio page, or check their social media profile.

### Check for FENSA registration

Ensure the company is registered with FENSA, a government scheme that monitors the replacement of windows and doors in England and Wales, and compliant with building regulations. This will save you time and money, as the company will register your window installation on your behalf. You’ll also have peace of mind knowing the company has undergone thorough vetting. Once the company finishes your double glazing installation, it should provide a FENSA certificate.

### Look into security measures

Ensure that your installer provides security measures, such a multipoint lock system, to protect your home, as windows can be an easy access point for burglars.

### Ask for the length of guarantees

Longer guarantee periods are better, and UK glaziers must offer a minimum 10-year guarantee on their labour and materials.

### Collect quotes from different installers

Check that the installer provides a free, no-obligation quote. While it’s common for companies to visit a home before providing a quote, they shouldn’t charge for every inspection. Transparent prices and value for money are important, so companies shouldn’t hide extra costs in the fine print.

## How to get a double glazing quote

The easiest way to get quotes from trusted companies for double glazing installations is to fill in our [free online quote form](https://fmb.secure-quote.com/windows-quotes/?utm_source=https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/best-double-glazing-companies/&utm_medium=MP&utm_campaign=Windows&utm_content=CTA%20Button). The process is simple:

1. Provide a few details, such as your contact information and how many windows you need to install.
2. Speak to our trusted partners, who will follow up your enquiry with a phone call or email (depending on your location). They will discuss your project in greater detail and provide free, no-obligation quotes.
3. If you accept one of the quotes, arrange a time for a specialist to visit your home. This specialist will measure your windows so the company can provide you with a more accurate price and ensure it manufactures all windows to your property’s exact specifications.
4. Arrange a time for the company to install your new windows.

## How to compare double glazing quotes

We recommend seeking quotes from both national and local double glazing installers, though it’s worth noting some national companies may subcontract the job to a local installer. This will help you to compare prices, including the cost of labour.

To ensure you receive an accurate cost estimate, make sure you have the answer to the following questions ready before contacting installers.

- How many windows do you want to install? You may only have 1 or 2 faulty windows, but if your other units are getting old, consider upgrading them too, as this could reduce installation costs in the long run.
- What style(s) of windows do you want (for example, tilt and turn, [casement](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/casement-windows/), sash or bay)?
- What window frame material do you want?
- What type of glass do you want?
- What sizes are your windows (roughly)?
- Is there anything about your house that could affect installation? For example, does it have a listed status that may need planning permission and could affect the kind of windows you can install?

When it comes to your installation timeline, it pays to be flexible, as double glazing companies tend to be busier at certain times of the year. As you might have to wait for your installation, the earlier you book your job, the better.

When discussing a double glazing quote, ask some questions to establish the company’s reliability and expertise. For example:

- What guarantees come with the windows?
- Does the company have relevant qualifications and experience?
- Is the installer a member of any professional associations?
- Can you see examples of any other projects the company has worked on?
- Will you receive follow-up support once the windows are fitted?
- Can you see the company’s insurance documents?
- Are the installer’s fitters paid a day rate or on a per-window basis? (this matters because installers paid a day rate may try to draw out the installation time to get paid more)

![Icon of a female expert](https://duf3nm0bhk54y.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/icons8-popular-man-50.png)

Expert tips: Navigating the quote process

We spoke to Rebecca Orde, a Project Manager specialising in residential development; she frequently obtains quotes for double glazing windows on behalf of her clients.

According to Rebecca, it’s vital to get a detailed breakdown of what’s involved in a double glazing installation and any associated costs.

“You’ll find with most medium-sized double glazing companies that they have the software to provide really detailed quotes, including specifications such as U-value, size, material, the gas used between the panes and so on. It’s really helpful getting that in-depth overview as well as a price,” she says.

Rebecca recommends obtaining quotes from several FENSA- or CERTASS-approved installers.

“Obtaining 2 or 3 quotes from different installers is ideal for most customers, assuming you’re talking to companies of known quality,” she says.

“Sometimes, you might not receive a response from certain installers. In some locations, there’s a high demand for building work, making it a seller’s market.”

## What are double glazing windows and how do they work?

![How does double glazing work: double glazing cross section](https://duf3nm0bhk54y.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/DoubleGlazingCrossSection.jpg.webp)

Knowing how [double glazing works](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/how-double-glazing-works/) can be helpful when you’re looking for a company to install your windows.

Double glazed windows provide excellent insulation and effective draught prevention. They are made of 2 panes of glass separated by spacer bars filled with air or gas, allowing daylight to enter and warm your home. The heat then transforms into infrared energy that can’t escape back through the glass, similar to the greenhouse effect. This process, known as solar gain, helps reduce your energy bills and your carbon footprint by minimising the need for extra heating.

### Double glazing energy efficiency grades explained

![icons8-lightbulb-100](https://duf3nm0bhk54y.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/icons8-lightbulb-100.png.webp)

All windows have an energy efficiency rating from the [British Fenestration Rating Council](https://www.bfrc.org/), which considers every component of double glazing in its assessment, including the glass used, the gas filling the spacing between panes and the manufacturing quality of the unit.

According to the latest building regulations in the UK, all new windows should be grade C or higher, but that shouldn’t be an issue as most new windows from top providers should be rated A and above.

### How much do double glazing windows cost in the UK?

Double glazing windows cost upwards of £500 for a single uPVC casement unit. However, the cost depends on the company you choose, the number of windows you need and the styles you prefer. As uPVC windows are made from engineered plastic, they are generally the most affordable option. The next cheapest is aluminium, and timber is typically the most expensive.1.0326

Considering the average UK property has 10 windows, these costs can quickly add up. The table below outlines the approximate costs of different materials and sizes of casement windows. On average, tilt and turn windows cost 25 per cent more than casement windows, and sash windows cost 50 per cent more.

| Casement window frame material | 600mm x 900mm | 900mm x 1,200mm | 1,200mm x 1,200mm |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| uPVC | £520 | £590 | £650 |
| Aluminium | £1,050 | £1,195 | £1,300 |
| Timber | £1,580 | £1,790 | £1,950 |

Prices reviewed in April 2026.

This calculator works using pricing data sourced directly from UK windows manufacturers. Prices are subject to fluctuations, however, so please use the results as a guide and contact suppliers for an exact price for your property.

### Double glazing prices, savings and finance

In this video, we explain the benefits of double glazing and the savings you can achieve by choosing it. Watch for advice on how to get financial help to pay for an installation and what to do if you need to fix or replace your windows.

![](https://embed-ssl.wistia.com/deliveries/bd7fe706bae1078ab73433e26de089a9.jpg)

## Double glazing window companies FAQs

### Who regulates double glazing companies in the UK?

In the UK, organisations like [FENSA](https://www.fensa.org.uk/) and [Certass](https://www.certass.co.uk/) regulate double glazing companies by certifying installers to ensure they meet building regulations and performance standards. Accreditation from either body signals a company’s compliance with safety and legal requirements.

### Should my double glazing installer fix problems with my windows after installation?

Under the [Consumer Rights Act 2015](https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2015/15/contents), double glazing companies must supply windows as described. The products must be of satisfactory quality and the installations carried out with reasonable care. If standards aren’t met, customers can request repairs or replacements. Reputable companies tend to provide warranties exceeding 10 years, but terms may vary, so always review the guarantee period before accepting a double glazing quote.

### What happens if my windows company goes into administration?

Several top UK double glazing installers – including Safestyle and Everest – have recently entered administration, creating uncertainty when it comes to the aftercare of their installations. While the Anglian group has incorporated these companies, if a company has no buyer to assume its obligations, guarantees may become void. However, you might still be able to make claims through your insurer if you have an Insurance Backed Guarantee policy.

### Can window companies install secondary glazing?

If you’re on a budget, [secondary glazing](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/secondary-glazing-windows/) can be a more affordable option than double glazing, as it adds glazed units to existing windows. While it offers less insulation and security, it excels at soundproofing, sometimes outperforming double glazing in that regard. Most of the companies in this guide do offer secondary glazing as well as double and triple glazing.

### Should I buy double or triple glazing when installing new windows?

As [triple glazing](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/triple-glazed-windows/) is around 10 to 20 per cent more expensive than double glazing, your budget will influence which you choose. It’s worth noting that despite the higher price, triple glazing’s additional pane and air-locked spaces improve energy efficiency, can potentially increase your savings by up to 50 per cent, making it a tempting choice for homeowners seeking enhanced insulation.

## How we assess the best window companies

To assess double glazing companies in the UK, we provide each with a score based on the following factors:

- the types of double glazing materials available – such as uPVC, aluminium or wood;
- the number of styles available;
- the company’s energy-efficiency rating;
- the level of security provided;
- the duration of the warranty;
- the company’s years of experience;
- the number of supported payment options;
- the company’s score on Trustpilot; and
- the company’s level of customer support; and
- any accreditations the company holds.

Read more about [how we review windows](https://www.fmb.org.uk/homepicks/windows/how-we-review-windows/).

Her previous experience includes heading up the social media channels for Wonderland in 2019, before joining New York Magazine’s The Strategist UK and researching consumer trends. She interviewed a range of professionals in the interior design space, as well as trend forecasters and shopping experts.

Following this, Hannah was the UK Shopping Writer for Woman and Home Digital, writing buying guides, reviews and other features; she oversaw all of the site’s shopping content and focused in particular on homes and lifestyle writing and editing.

![Workman installing a window.](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/AdobeStock_307058274.jpeg?width=744&height=503&format=webply)We take a look at the best window installers in the UK

Double glazing is one of the best ways to improve your home’s energy efficiency and reduce energy bills. But as our article on [**double glazing prices**](https://www.theecoexperts.co.uk/windows/double-glazing-costs) reveals, it costs upwards of £10,000 to replace all the windows on a typical house, so you’ll want to hire the best company for the job.

To help you get your money’s worth, we set out to find the best double-glazing companies in the UK. After analysing current information about hundreds of firms, including big names such as Everest, Anglian, and Safestyle as well as dozens of regional providers, we chose the best seven. We review them below.

We prioritised installers certified by [FENSA](https://www.fensa.org.uk/) (Fenestration Self-Assessment Scheme) and the [BFRC](https://www.bfrc.org/) (British Fenestration Rating Council). Our judging criteria also included warranties, experience, and choice of windows and other products. Crucially, all the companies in our list below receive good customer feedback.

See **our methodology** for more information on how we selected companies for our rundown.

### The best double-glazing companies at a glance

Here’s a quick look at how the UK’s seven best double-glazing installers compare on window guarantees, experience, and geographical coverage.

| Double glazing company | Guarantee | Founded | Area covered |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Anglian](https://www.anglianhome.co.uk/) | 10 years | 1966 | Mainland UK |
| [Everest](https://www.everest.co.uk/) | 10 – 20 years | 1965 | Mainland UK |
| [Safestyle](https://www.safestyle-windows.co.uk/) | 10 – 15 years | 1992 | Mainland UK |
| [First Home Improvements](https://www.firsthomeimprovements.co.uk/windows/) | 12 years | Early 1970’s | UK |
| [Britelite Windows](https://www.britelitewindows.co.uk/) | 10 years | 1970 | UK |
| [CR Smith](https://www.crsmith.co.uk/) | 10 years | 1917 (glazing since 1970s) | Scotland |
| [Clearview Home Improvements](https://www.clearviewhome.co.uk/) | 20 years | 2008 | North West |

### **What happened to Safestyle and Everest?**

The last few years have seen big changes in the double-glazing industry, with some companies sadly going out of business. [Safestyle](https://www.anglianhome.co.uk/news/administrators-of-safestyle-uk-agree-contract-with-anglian), for example, went into administration in October 2023, followed by [Everest](https://www.anglianhome.co.uk/news/anglian-home-improvements-completes-transaction-to-acquire-everest-2020-assets#:~:text=Anglian%20Home%20Improvements%20completes%20transaction%20to%20acquire%20Everest%202020%20assets) in April 2024. The brands and customer lists of both were bought by Anglian.

Everest, Safestyle and Anglian all still trade as separate brands, but they have the same parent company – Anglian Home Improvements (ASHI Group Ltd) – and share the same office in Norwich.

What does this mean for you? On the surface, not a lot. The only obvious development is that the three companies now offer the same financing options. You’re even greeted by an identical chat assistant on their Finance pages.

Anglian has worked to honour the orders of existing customers where possible. It does mean that warranties from previous Safestyle and Everest may not be valid, although as long as your installation was registered with FENSA you’ll still have recourse to an insurance-backed guarantee.

The three companies still have their own websites, product ranges, prices, accreditations and installers. However, we will keep a close eye on their customer feedback to ensure they’re keeping up more than just appearances.

## **Best choice of windows: Anglian**

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Anglian.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

Anglian is now the dominant firm in doubling glazing in the UK, so we’re not surprised to find it offering the best range of windows our researchers came across.

If you’re keen to upgrade your beloved sash windows or unusual wood frames with made-to-measure timber, uPVC, or aluminium replacements, this is the company to try first.

Anglian Home Improvements, to give it its full name, launched just after Everest in 1966 and offers all the standard window types, including casement, bay, “tilt & turn” and sash. You can also opt for custom shapes, etched glass and cottage windows that meet tight rules in conservation areas and listed buildings. Anglian even lets you order bespoke blinds to match your windows.

_**If you want to know how much new windows might set you back, look at our [double glazing cost calculator](https://www.theecoexperts.co.uk/windows/double-glazing-costs)**_.

The website has some excellent free tools to help you visualise various window types on your home, plus an inspiration gallery and guides to home renovation. Anglian’s site is less transparent on pricing than Everest’s, and its timber frames get very pricey. However, its quotes are said by some customers to match or undercut other companies.

Anglian holds a Trustpilot score of 3.9 based on approximately 35,000 reviews. Although they have a huge volume of reviews – nearly 35,000 at the time of writing, with close to 60% at the maximum five stars. Recent feedback indicates a high volume of positive experiences, alongside specific complaints regarding communication.

Pros

- Huge range of window styles and materials means you will find the ideal fit for your home

- Prices more affordable than Everest, except for timber frames

- Higher than average number of accreditations including FENSA, GGF and TrustMark

- Giant company, likely to be around for a long time – which is reassuring if you take out finance


Cons

- Customers often cite poor communication and aftercare, which may be a side effect of the company's large size

- Prices broadly similar to rivals but timber frames are expensive


#### **What Anglian’s customers say**

_“They gave the best quote of five other companies, and one company couldn’t even match Anglian before they made some discounts. They fitted two weeks ahead of schedule, and the installer knew exactly what he was doing”_ – [Martin Spaven, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66ce20516b7b62fa0369bd0a)

_“I had some issues to manage as windows were going in a new hipped roof, but Anglian bent over backwards to be flexible and work around the roofer. The windows were installed to a high standard and look very good”_ – [Claire Briault, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66c995ce5f39235c95cafc24)

## Best one-stop shop: Everest

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

Everest is our top choice for a ‘one-stop shop’ because it offers a complete range of home renovation products, including garage doors, conservatories, and roofline products, alongside its windows.

Everest’s 60-year history and comprehensive product range support its reputation. Everest is the most experienced firm of its kind, having begun to produce and fit aluminum secondary glazing in 1965. It later added uPVC and timber frames across the full range of window styles.

The company has been through a couple of incarnations, having gone into administration in 2020 (when it rose again as Everest 2020) and then in April 2024 before the takeover by Anglian. It also offers doors (including garage doors), conservatories, and roofline products such as guttering, adding to a convenient one-stop shop when renovating your home.

We found Everest’s website particularly easy to navigate, with clear information about guarantees, financing, and prices. Unlike companies on this list, Everest displays price estimates online rather than forcing you to submit details for a quote.

Prices start at £600 per uPVC casement window and rise to more than £2,000 if you opt for extras such as A++ rated triple glazing, custom frame colours, and a wealth of security features such as “GrabLock” locks. These extras can boost the value of your property and reduce your insurance premiums, but they are a sizeable upfront investment.

Everest receives a pretty average score of 3.8 on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/www.everest.co.uk), with some concerns about customer service.

**Read our in-depth** [**Everest review**](https://www.theecoexperts.co.uk/windows/everest-windows)

Pros

- Price estimates are offered upfront, although you'll need a quote for proper planning

- A++ rated triple glazing option offered as standard, for top-notch insulation and soundproofing

- Guarantees range from 10 years on most products to 15 years on sealed units


Cons

- Reviews sometimes mention unreliable service

- Finance options are less eye-catching than they were before Everest joined the Anglian family

- It may be a little soon to fully judge the impact of the takeover by Anglian on its customer service


#### **What Everest’s customers say**

_“Kept us well informed every step of the way. From first meeting to completion of work all involved were courteous and helpful, and answered any questions we had. Fitters were very efficient and treated us and our home with respect”_ – [Mr and Mrs Clark, April 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/660ed50611e4fbdc5797bab3)

_“Had secondary glazing installed for sound proofing and its made a big difference. Windows all look great, fitted well, had some very minor issues they were quick to come in, inspect and resolve with very little hassle”_ – [James, March 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/65e8c3475e1fd2fad8999fab)

### What do energy ratings mean?

Your home’s energy rating tells you how expensive it will be to heat and light your home. A home’s energy rating is measured from A, which is very efficient, to G, which is very inefficient. Your home’s rating will be on the Energy Performance Certificate (EPC), and this will also include what your rating could be if you made improvements, such as installing insulation, double glazing, solar panels or heat pumps.

Aluminium frame

## **Best for sustainability: Safestyle**

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest-2.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

Safestyle is our top pick for sustainability because it actively recycles old windows into drainpipes and converts waste wood into biomass fuel.

The Safestyle range isn’t as broad as Anglian’s or Everest’s, and its windows are all uPVC rather than aluminium or timber. But it also offers doors and roofline, and its windows cover a decent range of styles, including sash, leaded, etched, and French windows.

Safestyle’s commitment to sustainability genuinely impressed us. The company recycles your old windows and re-uses what it can to make new windows and other products. Every month, it recycles tonnes of used uPVC into drainpipes and plastic decking, turning wood into pellet fuel for biomass heating systems.

Like Anglian, Safestyle doesn’t publish any prices upfront. However, it offers a quote-match promise and a guarantee of up to 15 years on its windows.

Regarding customer reviews, it receives a relatively average score of 3.9 on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/safestyle-windows.co.uk), with some of the most recent reviews since the takeover by Anglian raising concerns about customer service and quality.

**Read our in-depth** [**Safestyle review**](https://www.theecoexperts.co.uk/windows/safestyle-windows)

Pros

- Safestyle pledges to meet any like-for-like quote

- Clear double-glazed units come with a 15-year guarantee

- The company takes away your old windows for recycling, so you don't have to worry about disposal or waste


Cons

- Safestyle is no longer an independent company following its rescue from administration by Anglian

- Only makes uPVC frames, not aluminium or timber


**What Safestyle’s customers say**

_“One workman arrived who was polite and respectful. He proceeded to remove five windows, fit the new ones and all their trim, clean (the) wreckage up all by himself… gone before dinner with no breaks. Brilliant!”_ – [Christine de Hamel, July 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66a8c70cca03d3f9335a0228)

_“The windows and doors fitted all look great and are really good quality. We liked the fact they are made in the UK too”_ – [Tamsyn, October 2023](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/653e91aa0557a9cd42f8840a)

![Workman installing a window.](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/AdobeStock_307058274.jpeg?width=104&height=104&fit=crop&format=webply)

Find out how much new windows would cost for your home today


Simply fill in our form and a trusted installer will be in touch with an accurate quote


[Get your quote now](https://quotes.theecoexperts.co.uk/org-windows?ctaType=cta-card-primary&text=Get+your+quote+now&touchpointId=774ce266-c74b-46ab-b440-1c68356dc4fe&deviceCategory=desktop&component=cta-card&position=1&totalPositions=1&instance=1)

## Best for security: First Home Improvements

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest-3.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

[First Home Improvements](https://www.firsthomeimprovements.co.uk/) will make any window you want in any style, as long as it has a uPVC frame. There are no wood frames here, alas. The company also installs conservatories, porches, and doors, including composite front doors, which you can plan online using the website’s [Door Designer](https://www.firsthomeimprovements.co.uk/doors/doordesigner/) tool.

All these products will enhance the security of your home, but First Home Improvements goes the extra mile by including Yale’s patented Blade Lock system – which it claims is “the strongest, safest and most secure lock on the market” – with all its windows and doors.

First Home Improvements guarantees the entire window, including locks and frames, for an unusually generous 12 years, compared with the typical 10 years for anything other than the sealed glazed unit. Customer reviews were among the best, up to an “excellent” 4.6 average on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/firsthomeimprovements.co.uk).

Pros

- Windows and doors use Yale Blade Locks, claimed to be the strongest window locks on the market

- Windows and locks guaranteed for 12 years, compared with the usual 10

- Long list of accreditations includes FENSA, BFRC, GGF and Made in Britain

- Excellent customer reviews praise fast, polite, tidy installers


Cons

- Only makes uPVC frames, not aluminium or timber


#### **What First Home Improvements’ customers say**

_“The installers turned up on time and were friendly, getting on with the job immediately. They worked non-stop until it was complete, doing a very professional job including taking away the old windows”_ – [John Adams, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66c75fe6af05585af6fb0f1c)

_“When installation was complete, all window locks and opening/closing functions were explained thoroughly. A very good job done very well”_ – [PB, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66bf8bbaffdd776a5db4030e)

## **Best on a budget: Britelite Windows**

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest-4.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

[Britelite](https://www.britelitewindows.co.uk/) is another company that doesn’t publish prices upfront. Still, when we asked for a free quote, we were pleasantly surprised by the result. Our research indicates that a Britelite uPVC casement window is approximately 50% cheaper than the equivalent Everest model. What makes this even more impressive is that all windows come with a 10-year guarantee that’s fully transferable if you sell your house.

We haven’t taken the quote process any further, so it’s hard to judge whether you get what you pay for with Britelite’s windows. However, customer reviews mention “superb quality” and say the windows “stay looking good for a lifetime.”

Customer reviews aren’t as glowing for Briteline as for other companies on our list (notably First Home Improvements, Clearview, and Scottish company CR Smith). That said, it still scores a respectable 4.5 on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/www.britelitewindows.co.uk), praising the company’s quick responses and professional approach.

Pros

- Prices are at the affordable end, but you will have to submit your details and wait for a reply to get a quote

- 10-year guarantees are fully transferable to subsequent openers of your home

- Range includes aluminium frames and triple glazing as well as double glazed uPVC

- Also installs doors, conservatories, flat roofing and roofline


Cons

- Customer reviews occasionally cite installation errors, and Britelite doesn't respond to all comments


#### **What Britelite’s customers say**

_“Luke and Paul came to install my bay window. Clean, efficient, very friendly and finished the job on time too. Definitely recommend to anyone wanting windows that last and stay looking good for a lifetime”_ – [Ana Partlett, July 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/668d34555f295cbe9c795967)

_“The workmen were clean and efficient, they worked hard, were diligent and tidied up everyday they were in the house. The windows are superb quality”_ – [Liza Stubbs, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66c82579b0147f2dfb96f68a)

## **Best for 3D previews: CR Smith Windows**

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest-5.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

Many double-glazing companies we researched offer 3D preview tools to help you visualise how your new windows, door, or conservatory will look in your home. But none offer quite the design process that Scottish firm [CR Smith](https://www.crsmith.co.uk/) does.

During CR Smith’s “virtual appointment,” the consultant uses photos and measurements of your property to create an immersive preview of how your home’s windows or conservatory will look. They talk you through the options available and suggest alternatives if you’re unhappy.

The company provides and installs Lorimer windows in many different finishes and styles. You must choose between uPVC (the cheapest) and timber (the most expensive) rather than opt for anything in between. If you go for those gorgeous wooden frames, you can at least spread the cost via the company’s finance options.

With customer feedback averaging a superb [4.9 on Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/crsmith.co.uk), we can only find one significant downside with CR Smith Windows: you have to live in Scotland to use them. Another reason to move north of the border, perhaps?

Pros

- Outstanding customer feedback is among the best we've seen for any firm

- Fully immersive digital preview of your project lets you check and tweak your choices before you commit

- Security guarantee promises to replace any any window frame broken by an intruder

- Financing is handled in-house rather than by a separate credit broker


Cons

- Only available in Scotland


#### **What CR Smith’s customers say**

_“Fantastic service from start to finish. We were kept informed every step of the way. The installation team were professional and friendly throughout, hardly knew they were there. Extremely happy with our new window, great quality and value for money!”_ – [KW, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66bd0e727912c0c5a8deefcb)

_“We wanted a company that we could trust and that’s what we got. The service from start to finish was great, the team are easily accessible by phone and email which made planning simple”_ – [Rachael, July 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/669f75769ef32433a504d8bd)

![Workman installing a window.](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/AdobeStock_307058274.jpeg?width=104&height=104&fit=crop&format=webply)

## **Best for warranty: Clearview Home Improvements**

![](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/sites/3/2015/08/Everest-6.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

The average double-glazing guarantee is around 10 years, roughly a third of the expected lifetime of a window, but Clearview doubles that with a 20-year warranty. This market-leading pledge is one of the reasons the company has won the industry’s [G Award](https://www.clearviewhome.co.uk/awards/) two years in a row.

Clearview’s website and customer feedback have a strong focus on living spaces, including conservatories and extensions rather than windows. Still, the company offers a decent range of window styles, albeit only in uPVC and aluminium.

As with CR Smith, the key limitation with Preston-based Clearview is geographical coverage: North West England, in this case. However, this is only a downside if you don’t live there.

If you do, you will benefit from the company’s local knowledge and teams – as confirmed by the firm’s excellent customer feedback, adding up to a strong 4.5 score on [Trustpilot](https://uk.trustpilot.com/review/www.clearviewhome.co.uk).

Pros

- Market-leading 20-year warranty on windows and other products, plus an outstanding 10-year labour guarantee to cover workmanship

- Superb range of home improvements including orangeries and extensions as well as windows and doors

- Windows made in the UK by manufacturing partner Conservatory Outlet

- Customer feedback is excellent, regularly citing great communication


Cons

- Coverage is currently limited to North West England, but that may change in time


#### **What Clearview’s customers say**

_“Each stage ran like clockwork with no delays including when the roof and windows were fitted in a mini monsoon… Not only thrilled with the end result but extremely impressed with the individual and collective work, and definitely the end product”_ – [John Barnard, August 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/66d089ab20d49aef2a8658cc)

_“Clearview were excellent from start to finish, from the initial design stage to final completion. We encountered delays outside their control, and they were very patient with us until we were in a position to proceed”_ – [Keith Mcguire, April 2024](https://uk.trustpilot.com/reviews/6617a844196f77e37e8cc79f)

## **How did we choose the best double glazing companies?**

![A window installer](https://images.theecoexperts.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2015/08/the-best-double-glazing-companies_window-installer.jpg?width=744&height=419&format=webply)

Our team of four researchers spent 28 hours investigating double-glazing companies to find the top seven firms across the country. Here are the key criteria we used to whittle the list down to seven:

- **Warranty –** windows need a solid warranty to protect them from unavoidable wear and tear. The average double glazing warranty is 10 years, with only the sealed units typically guaranteed for longer, so we prioritised companies offering more extended warranties.
- **Choice –** one window size does not fit all houses, and not everyone wants boring white uPVC. So we looked for companies that offered a wide range of materials and styles and made all their windows to measure.
- **Customer feedback –** we couldn’t test our best double-glazing companies by hiring them, so we turned to our most trusted sources: customers. We analysed nearly 1,000 verified customer reviews via TrustPilot and Check-a-Trade and prioritised companies with many positive reviews.
- **Customer service –** we gave companies credit for responding quickly and graciously to feedback and offering easy ways to get in touch. Some of the companies we considered for this article had customer service email addresses that immediately “bounced back,” so we decided not to include them.
- **Accreditation –** don’t even consider hiring a window installer in England and Wales who can’t give you a FENSA certificate because it will make your house harder to sell (take it from one who’s experienced it!). We also prioritised companies accredited by the TrustMark and the Glass and Glazing Federation.
- **Industry experience –** companies with 50 years of experience aren’t necessarily better than those with five years of experience. Still, if a firm has a long and successful history of providing and fitting windows, you can be reasonably confident that it knows its stuff.
- **Security –** double glazing naturally enhances the security of your home, but we wanted to see what else companies could offer for extra peace of mind and potentially lower insurance premiums. For example, security glass, complex locking mechanisms, and break-in guarantees.
- **Previews –** ideally, you want to know how windows will look in your home before you commit, so we looked for clever preview tools such as 3D virtual consultations that give customers a realistic idea of the finished job.
- **Affordability –** we didn’t just look for the cheapest windows overall but instead considered a balance of cost, quality, and transparency about pricing.
- **Finance options –** following Anglian’s purchase of Everest and Safestyle, there’s less competition in financing for home improvements, and interest rates have risen in recent years. We looked for companies offering easy, flexible repayments.
- **Geographical coverage –** we awarded star ratings for wide UK coverage because there’s little point in raving about a double glazing company whose locality means you may be unable to use them. That said, we recommend considering installers who are local to you. They will benefit from local knowledge and may be able to book you more quickly. Use our free double-glazing [**quote tool**](https://quotes.theecoexperts.co.uk/org-windows/) to find installers near you.

### What is a FENSA certificate and why is it so important?

A FENSA certificate is a document that shows your windows were installed by a registered company and that they meet the energy efficiency and safety Building Regulations. It also shows that your window installation has been reported to your local authority, which is a legal requirement.

A FENSA certificate is also needed for conveyancing and can make your home more valuable if you choose to sell it.

## Next steps

By now, you’ll have a good idea of which double-glazing installer is right for you. The next step is to find the best deal – and that’s where we come in.

Enter a few details about your home on our [**window quotes form**](https://quotes.theecoexperts.co.uk/org-windows/), and let us do the rest. We’ll pass on your details to our hand-picked professional installers, who will then get in touch to let you know their best prices. You have no obligation to take them up on it.

If you want to let some light in through a window in your roof, look at our guide to buying [**skylights and rooflights**](https://www.theecoexperts.co.uk/windows/skylights-and-rooflights).

## Summary

- We reviewed over 200 FENSA-certified double-glazing companies in the UK to identify the top seven installers.
- Our evaluation focused on 11 key factors, including warranties, customer feedback, product range, and accreditation.
- From national leaders like Anglian and Everest to regional specialists like CR Smith, we highlighted the best choices for different needs, including energy efficiency, security, and budget.
- Our guide reviews each company’s strengths and weaknesses, backed by verified customer feedback and industry expertise.
- Use our window quotes tool to connect with the best double-glazing professionals and find competitive pricing for your home improvement project.

Having double glazed windows installed in your home is now standard practice for most homes across the UK, thanks to leading double glazing companies making the installation affordable and straightforward. It’s one of the best ways to improve your home’s energy efficiency.

The thermal efficiency achieved helps modern UK homes become energy efficient in line with the government regulations that have been implemented to achieve net-zero by 2050. By opting for thermally efficient installations from a reputable double glazing company, the heat produced by your internal heating systems will work to a much greater effect. It keeps your home warmer for longer, so that you don’t have to spend inordinate amounts of money on your energy.

![Double Glazing Companies](https://www.doubleglazingontheweb.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/who-is-the-best-double-glazing-company-1-min.jpg)

The government is making a massive push for this to become the norm for houses around the UK because of the potential dangers of climate change and to save British households money on their energy because of the spikes globally in the cost of oil.

For those still thinking about having double glazing installed in their homes, or those who are looking to update their installations, we wanted to take some time today to look over the top companies out there at the moment who are providing double glazing installations and break down the pros and cons of each!

Here at Double Glazing on the Web, we want to make sure that our customers are as informed as we are when purchasing home renovations. We’re double glazing experts, and we believe sharing our expertise is important so customers can get the best deal available to them.

The main contenders we’ll be going through today are Safestyle, Everest and Anglian. We’ll be using each double glazing company’s Trustpilot rating at the time of writing, and identifying the main strength of each.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Company | Trustpilot | Strength |
| Safestyle | 3.9 | Offers Price Match Service |
| Anglian | 3.9 | Highest Window Quality |
| Everest | 3.6 | Best Warranty Period |

## **Safestyle**

Safestyle produces a variety of [uPVC window](https://www.doubleglazingontheweb.co.uk/upvc-windows/) styles and uPVC and composite doors, all fitted with modern double glazing. Don’t let the busy nature of the website put you off, it’s full of important info and competitive prices. This double glazing company is a reputable and well respected double glazing installer and has provided its services nationwide for over 25 years.

Safestyle is [**FENSA**](https://www.fensa.org.uk/) approved for its double glazing services and has a high customer satisfaction rating considering the sheer volume of customers they serve. Here is what one reviewer said:

### **_What are the main positives?_**

Many of the reviews echo the statement that Safestyle offers a brilliant service, and it can take a while perusing their Trustpilot before you find a wholly negative review. They do exist however, with every double glazing company, but they are in the vast minority when compared to the sheer amount of positive reviews.

Safestyle’s main positive is their price matching service in which they will match any like for like quote that you get from any other double glazing company for your services including sales prices. Here’s what they’re site says on the topic:

**“At Safestyle we know all about providing our customers with exceptional value – we’ve been doing it for years. To ensure this and so there’s never any doubt you’re getting the very best deal, we even promise to match any genuine quote.”**

There’s very little to say against Safestyle on this matter. They offer the best price, so long as you provide them with the price offer from their competitor, and they’re local capabilities are excellent. They have Safestyle installers stationed all over the country in all major cities. As a national pick, Safestyle is an excellent choice.

## **Anglian**

Anglian provides an extensive range of double glazing products, from windows, doors, conservatories, and more outside of double glazing. The website is well designed and offers a selection of pages that provide various pieces of information about double glazing and the other products that Anglian provides.

Anglian windows offer an energy efficiency rating across the board of A to A++, which is higher than most other companies’ B average. With this extra level of energy efficiency comes Anglian’s higher price point than the other major double glazing installers, however.

The Trustpilot reviews for Anglian are similar to Safestyle.

### **_Things to consider about Anglian double glazing services_**

Wait times are sometimes an issue with Anglian, which can be seen as both a positive and a negative. After all, if they’re busy then it must mean they’re doing something right, but lengthy wait times can mean you’re losing money with inefficient windows and doors. If you’re still using single pane windows with single glazing, then any time you have to wait for an upgrade to triple and double glazing options is money wasted on energy bills. Local installers will likely have shorter wait times, and competitive prices to boot.

## **Everest**

Like Anglian, Everest provides a wide variety of windows and doors fitted with double glazing and triple glazing from casement windows and sash windows to bay windows with aluminium frames and more. It is also fair to say that Everest is likely the most recognisable brand name of double glazing company for fitters in the United Kingdom. They have a wide network of local installers who take over the projects early in the planning stage so that customers feel that they are getting a local builder backed by a nationwide business.

All their products are made in Britain, and they claim to be at the forefront of double glazing products in the UK. There is little evidence to suggest that that claim is unfounded. They have many years of history and have become a household name.

Everest does, however have the lowest Trustpilot score from our options today. Of course, different customers have different experiences, but a more negative Trustpilot score is definitely a concern.

### **_Everest reviews_**

As a nationwide provider of double glazing services, Everest do have an impressive reputation for consistency of service. However, their most impressive strength is their warranty period which can be extended for up to 20 years, but they offer 10 years as standard. They also offer a premium service lifetime guarantee, which protects your new double glazing installation for life.

With Everest you can be certain that your new windows and doors are exceptional and are built to last.

## **Double Glazing On The Web: Who Is The Best Double Glazing Company?**

Here at Double Glazing on the Web, we consider ourselves experts in the UK’s vast network of double glazing installers. We can provide you with [the best prices from around the industry](https://www.doubleglazingontheweb.co.uk/double-glazing-cost/) for your projects with our expertise, and we also include both national and local installers to help you find the best possible price for your installation.

And don’t worry price isn’t all we’re concerned about. We look at your project and find the installers with the best reputation. Our entire network of installers are vetted by us and we ensure they have appropriate guarantees and accreditations to protect you.

Remember, whilst the three big names we’ve discussed today can offer a great service, they might not be the very best double glazing company for you. We can help you there.

If you would like to start a quote with us today, please feel free to use our quick and easy online pricing tool! We’re able to provide you with a comparison of accurate prices from around the nation so that you can skip all of the tedious research and get down to planning your double glazing installation!

We look forward to hearing from you!

## **Conclusion**

Each double glazing company has its own strengths, but don’t think just because they’re big names, they’re your only options. Smaller, local installers may have greater availability for a faster installation and be able to provide you with a more competitive price as they try to make an honest living without having to satisfy shareholders as some of the bigger companies will.

While all 3 companies normally have tremendous critiques on Trustpilot, it’s important to consider your specific wishes and budget whilst making a decision. By operating with an informed aid like Double Glazing at the Web, you could effortlessly compare fees and discover the best installer for your double glazing project.

## **FAQs**

### **What are the main benefits of installing double glazing in my home?**

Double glazing offers numerous benefits, along with stepped forward power efficiency, reduced noise pollutants, expanded domestic safety, and higher temperature management. By putting in double glazing, you can save money on your energy bills and maker your home more comfortable too.

### **How do I know which double glazing installer to pick out for my mission?**

When selecting a double glazing installer, don’t forget elements including the corporation’s popularity, client critiques, product quality, guarantee alternatives, and pricing.

It’s additionally useful to use a useful resource like Double Glazing at the Web, which could see you bag significant savings of up to 40% compared to comparing installers on your own.

### **Are there any incentives available for putting in double glazing?**

While there are presently no direct incentives for installing double glazing, having an energy-green domestic home will save you money and prepare your home for a greener future. Additionally, through enhancing your property’s energy performance, you’ll be contributing to the UK’s goal of reaching net-zero emissions by 2050.

### **How long does it commonly take to install double glazing?**

The installation time for double glazing can vary depending on the dimensions and complexity of your task. However, even the most complex installations may be completed inside a few days. Your chosen installer will provide you with a more accurate timeline when they know more about the project requirements.

### **What upkeep is needed for double glazing?**

Double glazing typically calls for minimum preservation, consisting of occasional cleansing with mild cleaning soap and water. The lifespan of double glazing can range from 20 to 35 years, depending on the materials and location of the windows and doors and what it’s exposed to, so they’re very low maintenance and require little input.

If you have any questions about this Privacy Statement, you can contact us by email at [info@leads2trade.co.uk](mailto:info@leads2trade.co.uk)  For the purposes of Data Protection legislation in the UK, we are a Data Controller. Our website is not intended, and we do not knowingly collect data relating to children.

This Privacy Notice is intended for:

- Our Website Visitors
- Our Consumer Customers
- Our Business Customers

## How do we process your personal data?

So that we can provide a service to you need to process some of your personal data.  We make sure that we only collect what we need and have outlined below the various ways in which we process your personal data:

**When you visit our website:** We use cookies on our website which collect various types of personal data. This will include details about your visits to our website; page views, downloads, navigation and exit; IP address; geographical location; browser type and version; operating system; referral source; length of your visit.

Some of these cookies are necessary to ensure that the website works correctly, and other cookies require your consent.  You can find out more about our cookies and how you can manage your consent in our [cookie policy here](https://www.doubleglazingontheweb.co.uk/cookie-policy/).

Our website may contain links to other external websites.  We aren’t responsible for the practices, policies or content of such websites and suggest that you check their privacy policies to ensure that you are happy to continue browsing.

**When you become one of our consumer customers:** Our lawful basis for processing data is consent. This is captured verbally through a phone call to yourself to confirm you are happy for your data, input into our webform, to be transferred to our trusted providers. All our calls are recorded for compliance checking.

We will process your name and contact details (phone number, email and postal address) so that we can put you in contact with Trusted suppliers who will be providing you with the home improvement quotes you have requested, answering any enquiries you may have and monitoring how the Trusted suppliers we work with are performing.

We will call you to check that we have the correct information and learn about how we can connect you with the best quotes in your geographical area. We may send you an SMS message if your enquiry is received outside our operational hours or to provide an update on the progress of your enquiry. An SMS opt will be provided after failure to connect via phone.

Our phone calls are recorded for training purposes however if you are uncomfortable with this, we will not proceed with the call and delete the recording. We also use aggregated and anonymised data to keep records for our own business use.

For certain home improvement enquiries where we have been unable to reach you by telephone, we will send you an email to introduce you to a maximum of two of our ‘Trusted Third Party Suppliers’ who may contact you directly to help you obtain a comparison quote for the specific home improvement measure. Your information is important to us, and we ensure our suppliers are established through reference and credit checks and certified to work in their relevant industries. Our Trusted partners will be registered with Gas Safe for the replacement of boilers, be a registered member of FENSA, CERTASS, ASSURE or the Consumer Protection Association (CPA) for fenestration products in England and Wales, and will offer insurance backed guarantees (IBG’s) in Scotland. All our Trusted renewable energy partners that offer quotations for solar panels and air source heat pumps will be registered with the Microgeneration Certification Scheme. (MCS)

Currently we work with over 300 trusted suppliers throughout the UK, examples of some of the suppliers are Heatable Ltd and Verisure Services UK Ltd. We are happy to share the names of any of our other partners who may contact you (maximum of two) to assist you in obtaining quotes on request.

**When you become one of our business customers:** We will process your name and business contact details (phone number, email and postal address) so that we register you and put you in touch with our consumer customers who have requested home improvements quotes from you.  Our contract is with your business however we will process the personal data of our business customers under our legitimate interest.  We have done a balancing test to ensure that we are processing your personal data fairly and respectfully.

**When you send us an enquiry:** You may have sent us an enquiry via the contact page on our website.  We will collect your name and contact details as it is in our **legitimate interest** to do so that we can enter into correspondence with you and answer your enquiry.

## How long do we keep your personal data?

**If you have consented to non-essential cookies:** You can find out more about our cookies and update your preference on our Cookies Consent Management Platform.

**If you are a consumer customer of ours:** we will keep your personal data for as long as we have business dealings with you and then for a further 12 months.  This is because the quotes that you obtain are valid for up to a period of 12 months and it is important for us to be able to assist with any enquiries you have during that time.

If you have consented to receive email marketing from us, we will keep your personal data until you unsubscribe. If you choose not to engage with our email marketing messages for a period of 12 months, we will not email you again and put your personal data on a suppression list.

**If you are a business customer of ours:** we will keep your personal data for as long as we have a contract with you and then for a further 7 years in accordance with HMRC guidelines.

## How do we use your personal data to communicate with you?

**Non-Marketing Communications include:** service messages regarding quotes or appointments from our third party services to enquiries and other communications to ensure that we deliver the services you expect from us.

If you are one of business customers, you will receive emails regarding your registration, services messages and invoices.  You will have access to our members area dashboard portal where you can find more information about your account details with us.

**Marketing Communications include:** Emails regarding other services we offer as well as content marketing we hope you will find useful. If you are one of our consumer customers, you will only receive email marketing from us if you have given us your consent. We do send out unsolicited email marketing messages to our business customers when we feel we have a legitimate interest to do so. You are welcome to unsubscribe from marketing messages at any time.

If you have consented to marketing cookies such as Facebook Pixels you may be served Facebook Ads regarding our services.  You can change your cookies consent at any time by visiting our Cookie Consent Management Platform.  You may also wish to change your Facebook settings if you don’t want to see targeted advertising.

**Social Media:** We use Facebook, and LinkedIn (for business purposes). We sometimes use paid advertising on these platforms, but we will only use custom audiences with your consent.  You can find a copy of this Privacy Notice on our Facebook page in the ‘About’ section.

**Social Plugins:** We use social plugins on our website pages which allow you to share products with your friends and families.  These buttons are deactivated by default and only activated once you click on them.  When you click on these buttons your personal data will be transferred to the plugin provider.  This will include IP addresses which are then used to serve you relevant ads.

## Who has access to your personal data?

We share your personal data with various organisations so we can fulfil our contract with you to help us run our business and to fulfil our legal obligations.

**These include:** Marketing Agencies, Accountants, Webhosts, IT and CRM Agencies. We also use Microsoft, DropBox, and Infonotes to ensure that we run our business efficiently.

**If you are one of our consumer customers:** we will share your details with the suppliers in your geographical area who are able to give you a quote for the work, you require.  Our suppliers are registered with us and we ensure that they are reputable and certified where appropriate.  We will discuss this with you in our phone call so that you are aware of what is happening every step of the way.

We ensure that our systems are robust, and we will only share your data with organisations who have appropriate safeguarding mechanisms in place such as Standard Contractual Clauses.  We have Data Processing Agreements with our Data Processors and never sell personal data to third parties unless we have your agreement to do so.

If Leads 2 Trade is sold or transfers its assets to another party, your personal data could be one of the transferred assets.  If your personal data is transferred it will remain subject to this Privacy Notice.  Your personal data will be passed onto a successor in the event of a liquidation or administration.

## What are your rights?

You have several rights which you can access free of charge.  In certain circumstances we reserve the right to charge a reasonable fee if your requests are unfounded or excessive.

You have the right:

- To be informed about the collection and use of your personal data.
- To access your personal data via a Data Subject Access Request (DSAR)
- To have inaccurate personal data corrected or completed
- To have your personal data erased
- To restrict our processing of your personal data
- To request the transfer of any personal data you have provided to us
- To object at any time to direct marketing
- To object to automated decision making and profiling

We don’t use any automated decision making.

For more information about these rights and when you can request them, please visit the [ICO website](https://ico.org.uk/).

If you wish to exercise any of these rights, please contact us by email at [info@leads2trade.co.uk](mailto:info@leads2trade.co.uk). We will respond to you within one month.  If your request is complex and we require more time, we will contact you and let you know within the first month.

## How can you submit a query or a complaint?

We are always happy to answer your queries regarding privacy.  Please contact us by telephone on [0800 124 4308](tel:+448001244308) or email [info@leads2trade.co.uk](mailto:info@leads2trade.co.uk).

If you have a complaint, please get in touch with us so that we can resolve any concerns.  You also have a right to lodge a complaint with the Information Commissioner’s Office if you believe your personal data has been processed in a way which does not comply with Data Protection Legislation.  You can call the ICO helpline on [0303 123 1113](tel:+443031231113) or [visit their website here](https://ico.org.uk/).

Changes to this Privacy Notice

We keep this Privacy Notice under regular review.  The most recent update is 29th Nov 2023
